---
title: "Using medfateland with SERGHEI"
author: "Miquel De Cáceres / Mario Morales / Daniel Caviedes-Voullieme"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
description: >
   Instructions to compile and link package medfateland with SERGHEI model
output: 
  rmarkdown::html_document:
    toc: TRUE
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Aim

SERGHEI (Simulation Environment for Geomorphology, Hydrodynamics and Ecohydrology in Integrated form) is a modelling environment distributed at https://gitlab.com/serghei-model/serghei. CRAN distribution of package `medfateland` does not include SERGHEI. Therefore, users willing to run watershed simulations (i.e. functions `spwb_land()`, `growth_land()` or `fordyn_land()`) along with SERGHEI, should compile the two models together. This vignette is intended to guide users throughout the installation process.

## 1. Downloading medfateland and SERGHEI sources

* medfateland branch
* SERGHEI branch

## 2. Building SERGHEI dynamic library


## 3. Compiling and linking package medfateland with SERGHEI

Makevars file:

```{bash, eval = FALSE}
# SERGHEI path configuration
SERGHEI_PATH = $(HOME)/Rpackages/medfateland/serghei

# Preprocessor flags: include SERGHEI headers and enable coupling
PKG_CPPFLAGS = -I$(SERGHEI_PATH)/src -DSERGHEI_MEDFATELAND=1

# Linker flags: link against libserghei.so with rpath
PKG_LIBS = -L$(SERGHEI_PATH)/lib -lserghei -Wl,-rpath,$(SERGHEI_PATH)/lib

# C++ standard
PKG_CXXFLAGS = -O3 -std=c++17
```

## 4. Testing
